tracks.title: The Stardust Engine
tracks.loreNote: The reclaimed 1987 anthem. Opening movement of The Stardust Suite, kicking down the door to their independent era.

tracks.title: Hard Reset
tracks.loreNote: Second movement of The Stardust Suite. A metaphor for surviving the vacuum of true independence after escaping corporate gravity.

tracks.title: Brand New Scene
tracks.loreNote: The resolved, peaceful conclusion to The Stardust Suite. The sound of an engine humming perfectly in the open vacuum.

tracks.title: Static on the Dial
tracks.loreNote: A quintessential driving anthem about the freedom of the open road, cementing them as a unified rock band.

tracks.title: The Fortress
tracks.loreNote: A pure 1980s rock anthem dedicated to their survival, canonizing manager Holly O'Connell as the architect of their fortress.

tracks.title: Inertia (Unstoppable)
tracks.loreNote: A high-BPM translation of Newtonian physics into a driving rock anthem, celebrating their unstoppable forward momentum.

tracks.title: Thunderhead
tracks.loreNote: A slow, thunderous, sludgy arena-rock track aimed directly at the pristine, over-produced sound of their Apex Records days.

tracks.title: Redline
tracks.loreNote: A frantic, technically demanding duel between Ryan's guitar and Cassidy's synth, pushing the band to the absolute edge of control.

tracks.title: Echoes in the Wire
tracks.loreNote: Replaced the traumatic 'If You Walked Away.' An 80s power ballad transforming past anxiety into a triumphant celebration of unity.

tracks.title: My Anchor (The Sail)
tracks.loreNote: A rewrite of their 1987 hit, shedding codependency for equality. A massive power ballad sailing into their Freedom Era.

tracks.title: Apoapsis
tracks.loreNote: First movement of The Apoapsis Suite. A pure ambient synth-rock instrumental representing the silent, zero-gravity vacuum of space.

tracks.title: Second Apoapsis
tracks.loreNote: Unbroken continuation of Apoapsis. Introduces the cinematic tension of deep-space exploration, building to a towering crescendo.

tracks.title: The Spectrum (True Color)
tracks.loreNote: The 7-minute finale to the suite. A sprawling, cosmic reimagining of their 1987 corporate pop hit, 'Electric Color'.

tracks.title: Moon 1 (Tidal Lock)
tracks.loreNote: Cassidy's masterpiece of 80s Cosmic Pop. Uses a tidal lock as a gorgeous metaphor for unwavering loyalty.

tracks.title: Moon 2 (Roche Limit)
tracks.loreNote: Ryan's dark, aggressive rock counterpart to Tidal Lock, using the Roche limit to describe a toxic, fatal attraction.

tracks.title: Escape Velocity (Ad Astra)
tracks.loreNote: Cassidy's 15-minute autobiographical progressive rock epic. Later used as coded rebel transmissions in the 2017 sci-fi film K.N.O.X.
